mojo.description: Package an application into an OCI image using a buildpack, forking the lifecycle to make sure that {@code package} ran. This goal is suitable for command-line invocation. If you need to configure a goal {@code execution} in your build, use {@code build-image-no-fork} instead.

parameter.name: applicationDirectory
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#applicationDirectory} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: classifier
parameter.description: Classifier used when finding the source archive.

parameter.name: cleanCache
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#cleanCache} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: createdDate
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#createdDate} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: docker
parameter.description: Docker configuration options.

parameter.name: excludeDevtools
parameter.description: Exclude Spring Boot devtools from the repackaged archive.

parameter.name: excludeDockerCompose
parameter.description: Exclude Spring Boot dev services from the repackaged archive.

parameter.name: excludeGroupIds
parameter.description: Comma separated list of groupId names to exclude (exact match).

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to exclude. The {@link Exclude} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: finalName
parameter.description: Name of the source archive.

parameter.name: image
parameter.description: Image configuration, with {@code builder}, {@code runImage}, {@code name}, {@code env}, {@code cleanCache}, {@code verboseLogging}, {@code pullPolicy}, and {@code publish} options.

parameter.name: imageBuilder
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#builder} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: imageName
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#name} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: includeSystemScope
parameter.description: Include system scoped dependencies.

parameter.name: includeTools
parameter.description: Include JAR tools.

parameter.name: includes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to include. The {@link Include} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: layers
parameter.description: Layer configuration with options to disable layer creation, exclude layer tools jar, and provide a custom layers configuration file.

parameter.name: layout
parameter.description: The type of archive (which corresponds to how the dependencies are laid out inside it). Possible values are {@code JAR}, {@code WAR}, {@code ZIP}, {@code DIR}, {@code NONE}. Defaults to a guess based on the archive type.

parameter.name: layoutFactory
parameter.description: The layout factory that will be used to create the executable archive if no explicit layout is set. Alternative layouts implementations can be provided by 3rd parties.

parameter.name: loaderImplementation
parameter.description: The loader implementation that should be used.

parameter.name: mainClass
parameter.description: The name of the main class. If not specified the first compiled class found that contains a {@code main} method will be used.

parameter.name: network
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#network} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The Maven project.

parameter.name: publish
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#publish} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: pullPolicy
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#pullPolicy} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: runImage
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#runImage} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: session
parameter.description: The Maven session.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution.

parameter.name: sourceDirectory
parameter.description: Directory containing the source archive.

mojo.description: Package an application into an OCI image using a buildpack, but without forking the lifecycle. This goal should be used when configuring a goal {@code execution} in your build. To invoke the goal on the command-line, use {@code build-image} instead.

parameter.name: applicationDirectory
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#applicationDirectory} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: classifier
parameter.description: Classifier used when finding the source archive.

parameter.name: cleanCache
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#cleanCache} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: createdDate
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#createdDate} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: docker
parameter.description: Docker configuration options.

parameter.name: excludeDevtools
parameter.description: Exclude Spring Boot devtools from the repackaged archive.

parameter.name: excludeDockerCompose
parameter.description: Exclude Spring Boot dev services from the repackaged archive.

parameter.name: excludeGroupIds
parameter.description: Comma separated list of groupId names to exclude (exact match).

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to exclude. The {@link Exclude} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: finalName
parameter.description: Name of the source archive.

parameter.name: image
parameter.description: Image configuration, with {@code builder}, {@code runImage}, {@code name}, {@code env}, {@code cleanCache}, {@code verboseLogging}, {@code pullPolicy}, and {@code publish} options.

parameter.name: imageBuilder
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#builder} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: imageName
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#name} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: includeSystemScope
parameter.description: Include system scoped dependencies.

parameter.name: includeTools
parameter.description: Include JAR tools.

parameter.name: includes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to include. The {@link Include} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: layers
parameter.description: Layer configuration with options to disable layer creation, exclude layer tools jar, and provide a custom layers configuration file.

parameter.name: layout
parameter.description: The type of archive (which corresponds to how the dependencies are laid out inside it). Possible values are {@code JAR}, {@code WAR}, {@code ZIP}, {@code DIR}, {@code NONE}. Defaults to a guess based on the archive type.

parameter.name: layoutFactory
parameter.description: The layout factory that will be used to create the executable archive if no explicit layout is set. Alternative layouts implementations can be provided by 3rd parties.

parameter.name: loaderImplementation
parameter.description: The loader implementation that should be used.

parameter.name: mainClass
parameter.description: The name of the main class. If not specified the first compiled class found that contains a {@code main} method will be used.

parameter.name: network
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#network} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The Maven project.

parameter.name: publish
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#publish} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: pullPolicy
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#pullPolicy} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: runImage
parameter.description: Alias for {@link Image#runImage} to support configuration through command-line property.

parameter.name: session
parameter.description: The Maven session.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution.

parameter.name: sourceDirectory
parameter.description: Directory containing the source archive.

mojo.description: Generate a {@code build-info.properties} file based on the content of the current {@link MavenProject}.

parameter.name: additionalProperties
parameter.description: Additional properties to store in the {@code build-info.properties} file. Each entry is prefixed by {@code build.} in the generated {@code build-info.properties}.

parameter.name: excludeInfoProperties
parameter.description: Properties that should be excluded {@code build-info.properties} file. Can be used to exclude the standard {@code group}, {@code artifact}, {@code name}, {@code version} or {@code time} properties as well as items from {@code additionalProperties}.

parameter.name: outputFile
parameter.description: The location of the generated {@code build-info.properties} file.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The Maven project.

parameter.name: session
parameter.description: The Maven session.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution.

parameter.name: time
parameter.description: The value used for the {@code build.time} property in a form suitable for {@link Instant#parse(CharSequence)}. Defaults to {@code project.build.outputTimestamp} or {@code session.request.startTime} if the former is not set. To disable the {@code build.time} property entirely, use {@code 'off'} or add it to {@code excludeInfoProperties}.

mojo.description:
Display help information on spring-boot-maven-plugin.
Call mvn spring-boot:help -Ddetail=true -Dgoal=<goal-name> to display parameter details.

parameter.name: detail
parameter.description: If true, display all settable properties for each goal.

parameter.name: goal
parameter.description: The name of the goal for which to show help. If unspecified, all goals will be displayed.

parameter.name: indentSize
parameter.description: The number of spaces per indentation level, should be positive.

parameter.name: lineLength
parameter.description: The maximum length of a display line, should be positive.

mojo.description: Invoke the AOT engine on the application.

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: Application arguments that should be taken into account for AOT processing.

parameter.name: classesDirectory
parameter.description: Directory containing the classes and resource files that should be packaged into the archive.

parameter.name: compilerArguments
parameter.description: Arguments that should be provided to the AOT compile process. On command line, make sure to wrap multiple values between quotes.

parameter.name: excludeGroupIds
parameter.description: Comma separated list of groupId names to exclude (exact match).

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to exclude. The {@link Exclude} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: generatedClasses
parameter.description: Directory containing the generated classes.

parameter.name: generatedResources
parameter.description: Directory containing the generated resources.

parameter.name: generatedSources
parameter.description: Directory containing the generated sources.

parameter.name: includes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to include. The {@link Include} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: jvmArguments
parameter.description: JVM arguments that should be associated with the AOT process. On command line, make sure to wrap multiple values between quotes.

parameter.name: mainClass
parameter.description: Name of the main class to use as the source for the AOT process. If not specified the first compiled class found that contains a 'main' method will be used.

parameter.name: profiles
parameter.description: Spring profiles to take into account for AOT processing.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The Maven project.

parameter.name: session
parameter.description: The current Maven session. This is used for toolchain manager API calls.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution.

parameter.name: systemPropertyVariables
parameter.description: List of JVM system properties to pass to the AOT process.

mojo.description: Invoke the AOT engine on tests.

parameter.name: classesDirectory
parameter.description: Directory containing the classes and resource files that should be used to run the tests.

parameter.name: compilerArguments
parameter.description: Arguments that should be provided to the AOT compile process. On command line, make sure to wrap multiple values between quotes.

parameter.name: excludeGroupIds
parameter.description: Comma separated list of groupId names to exclude (exact match).

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to exclude. The {@link Exclude} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: generatedClasses
parameter.description: Directory containing the generated test classes.

parameter.name: generatedResources
parameter.description: Directory containing the generated test resources.

parameter.name: generatedSources
parameter.description: Directory containing the generated sources.

parameter.name: generatedTestClasses
parameter.description: Directory containing the generated test classes.

parameter.name: includes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to include. The {@link Include} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: jvmArguments
parameter.description: JVM arguments that should be associated with the AOT process. On command line, make sure to wrap multiple values between quotes.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The Maven project.

parameter.name: session
parameter.description: The current Maven session. This is used for toolchain manager API calls.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution.

parameter.name: systemPropertyVariables
parameter.description: List of JVM system properties to pass to the AOT process.

parameter.name: testClassesDirectory
parameter.description: Directory containing the classes and resource files that should be packaged into the archive.

mojo.description: Repackage existing JAR and WAR archives so that they can be executed from the command line using {@literal java -jar}. With layout=NONE can also be used simply to package a JAR with nested dependencies (and no main class, so not executable).

parameter.name: attach
parameter.description: Attach the repackaged archive to be installed into your local Maven repository or deployed to a remote repository. If no classifier has been configured, it will replace the normal jar. If a {@code classifier} has been configured such that the normal jar and the repackaged jar are different, it will be attached alongside the normal jar. When the property is set to {@code false}, the repackaged archive will not be installed or deployed.

parameter.name: classifier
parameter.description: Classifier to add to the repackaged archive. If not given, the main artifact will be replaced by the repackaged archive. If given, the classifier will also be used to determine the source archive to repackage: if an artifact with that classifier already exists, it will be used as source and replaced. If no such artifact exists, the main artifact will be used as source and the repackaged archive will be attached as a supplemental artifact with that classifier. Attaching the artifact allows to deploy it alongside to the original one, see the Maven documentation for more details.

parameter.name: embeddedLaunchScript
parameter.description: The embedded launch script to prepend to the front of the jar if it is fully executable. If not specified the 'Spring Boot' default script will be used.

parameter.name: embeddedLaunchScriptProperties
parameter.description: Properties that should be expanded in the embedded launch script.

parameter.name: excludeDevtools
parameter.description: Exclude Spring Boot devtools from the repackaged archive.

parameter.name: excludeDockerCompose
parameter.description: Exclude Spring Boot dev services from the repackaged archive.

parameter.name: excludeGroupIds
parameter.description: Comma separated list of groupId names to exclude (exact match).

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to exclude. The {@link Exclude} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: executable
parameter.description:
Make a fully executable jar for *nix machines by prepending a launch script to the jar.

Currently, some tools do not accept this format so you may not always be able to use this technique. For example, {@code jar -xf} may silently fail to extract a jar or war that has been made fully-executable. It is recommended that you only enable this option if you intend to execute it directly, rather than running it with {@code java -jar} or deploying it to a servlet container.

parameter.name: finalName
parameter.description: Name of the generated archive.

parameter.name: includeSystemScope
parameter.description: Include system scoped dependencies.

parameter.name: includeTools
parameter.description: Include JAR tools.

parameter.name: includes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to include. The {@link Include} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: layers
parameter.description: Layer configuration with options to disable layer creation, exclude layer tools jar, and provide a custom layers configuration file.

parameter.name: layout
parameter.description: The type of archive (which corresponds to how the dependencies are laid out inside it). Possible values are {@code JAR}, {@code WAR}, {@code ZIP}, {@code DIR}, {@code NONE}. Defaults to a guess based on the archive type.

parameter.name: layoutFactory
parameter.description: The layout factory that will be used to create the executable archive if no explicit layout is set. Alternative layouts implementations can be provided by 3rd parties.

parameter.name: loaderImplementation
parameter.description: The loader implementation that should be used.

parameter.name: mainClass
parameter.description: The name of the main class. If not specified the first compiled class found that contains a {@code main} method will be used.

parameter.name: outputDirectory
parameter.description: Directory containing the generated archive.

parameter.name: outputTimestamp
parameter.description: Timestamp for reproducible output archive entries, either formatted as ISO 8601 (yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ssXXX) or an {@code int} representing seconds since the epoch.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The Maven project.

parameter.name: requiresUnpack
parameter.description: A list of the libraries that must be unpacked from uber jars in order to run. Specify each library as a {@code <dependency>} with a {@code <groupId>} and a {@code <artifactId>} and they will be unpacked at runtime.

parameter.name: session
parameter.description: The Maven session.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution.

parameter.name: addResources
parameter.description: Add maven resources to the classpath directly, this allows live in-place editing of resources. Duplicate resources are removed from {@code target/classes} to prevent them from appearing twice if {@code ClassLoader.getResources()} is called. Please consider adding {@code spring-boot-devtools} to your project instead as it provides this feature and many more.

parameter.name: additionalClasspathElements
parameter.description: Additional classpath elements that should be added to the classpath. An element can be a directory with classes and resources or a jar file.

parameter.name: agents
parameter.description: Path to agent jars.

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: Arguments that should be passed to the application.

parameter.name: classesDirectory
parameter.description: Directory containing the classes and resource files that should be used to run the application.

parameter.name: commandlineArguments
parameter.description: Arguments from the command line that should be passed to the application. Use spaces to separate multiple arguments and make sure to wrap multiple values between quotes. When specified, takes precedence over {@link #arguments}.

parameter.name: directories
parameter.deprecated: since 3.2.0 for removal in 3.4.0 in favor of 'additionalClasspathElements'
parameter.description: Additional directories containing classes or resources that should be added to the classpath.

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: List of Environment variables that should be associated with the forked process used to run the application.

parameter.name: excludeGroupIds
parameter.description: Comma separated list of groupId names to exclude (exact match).

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to exclude. The {@link Exclude} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: includes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to include. The {@link Include} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: jvmArguments
parameter.description: JVM arguments that should be associated with the forked process used to run the application. On command line, make sure to wrap multiple values between quotes.

parameter.name: mainClass
parameter.description: The name of the main class. If not specified the first compiled class found that contains a 'main' method will be used.

parameter.name: noverify
parameter.description: Flag to say that the agent requires -noverify.

parameter.name: optimizedLaunch
parameter.description: Whether the JVM's launch should be optimized.

parameter.name: profiles
parameter.description: The spring profiles to activate. Convenience shortcut of specifying the 'spring.profiles.active' argument. On command line use commas to separate multiple profiles.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The Maven project.

parameter.name: session
parameter.description: The current Maven session. This is used for toolchain manager API calls.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution.

parameter.name: systemPropertyVariables
parameter.description: List of JVM system properties to pass to the process.

parameter.name: useTestClasspath
parameter.description: Flag to include the test classpath when running.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: Current working directory to use for the application. If not specified, basedir will be used.

mojo.description: Start a spring application. Contrary to the {@code run} goal, this does not block and allows other goals to operate on the application. This goal is typically used in integration test scenario where the application is started before a test suite and stopped after.

parameter.name: addResources
parameter.description: Add maven resources to the classpath directly, this allows live in-place editing of resources. Duplicate resources are removed from {@code target/classes} to prevent them from appearing twice if {@code ClassLoader.getResources()} is called. Please consider adding {@code spring-boot-devtools} to your project instead as it provides this feature and many more.

parameter.name: additionalClasspathElements
parameter.description: Additional classpath elements that should be added to the classpath. An element can be a directory with classes and resources or a jar file.

parameter.name: agents
parameter.description: Path to agent jars.

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: Arguments that should be passed to the application.

parameter.name: classesDirectory
parameter.description: Directory containing the classes and resource files that should be used to run the application.

parameter.name: commandlineArguments
parameter.description: Arguments from the command line that should be passed to the application. Use spaces to separate multiple arguments and make sure to wrap multiple values between quotes. When specified, takes precedence over {@link #arguments}.

parameter.name: directories
parameter.deprecated: since 3.2.0 for removal in 3.4.0 in favor of 'additionalClasspathElements'
parameter.description: Additional directories containing classes or resources that should be added to the classpath.

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: List of Environment variables that should be associated with the forked process used to run the application.

parameter.name: excludeGroupIds
parameter.description: Comma separated list of groupId names to exclude (exact match).

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to exclude. The {@link Exclude} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: includes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to include. The {@link Include} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: jmxName
parameter.description: The JMX name of the automatically deployed MBean managing the lifecycle of the spring application.

parameter.name: jmxPort
parameter.description: The port to use to expose the platform MBeanServer.

parameter.name: jvmArguments
parameter.description: JVM arguments that should be associated with the forked process used to run the application. On command line, make sure to wrap multiple values between quotes.

parameter.name: mainClass
parameter.description: The name of the main class. If not specified the first compiled class found that contains a 'main' method will be used.

parameter.name: maxAttempts
parameter.description: The maximum number of attempts to check if the spring application is ready. Combined with the "wait" argument, this gives a global timeout value (30 sec by default)

parameter.name: noverify
parameter.description: Flag to say that the agent requires -noverify.

parameter.name: profiles
parameter.description: The spring profiles to activate. Convenience shortcut of specifying the 'spring.profiles.active' argument. On command line use commas to separate multiple profiles.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The Maven project.

parameter.name: session
parameter.description: The current Maven session. This is used for toolchain manager API calls.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution.

parameter.name: systemPropertyVariables
parameter.description: List of JVM system properties to pass to the process.

parameter.name: useTestClasspath
parameter.description: Flag to include the test classpath when running.

parameter.name: wait
parameter.description: The number of milliseconds to wait between each attempt to check if the spring application is ready.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: Current working directory to use for the application. If not specified, basedir will be used.

mojo.description: Stop an application that has been started by the "start" goal. Typically invoked once a test suite has completed.

parameter.name: jmxName
parameter.description: The JMX name of the automatically deployed MBean managing the lifecycle of the application.

parameter.name: jmxPort
parameter.description: The port to use to look up the platform MBeanServer.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The Maven project.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution.

mojo.description: Run an application in place using the test runtime classpath. The main class that will be used to launch the application is determined as follows: The configured main class, if any. Then the main class found in the test classes directory, if any. Then the main class found in the classes directory, if any.

parameter.name: addResources
parameter.description: Add maven resources to the classpath directly, this allows live in-place editing of resources. Duplicate resources are removed from {@code target/classes} to prevent them from appearing twice if {@code ClassLoader.getResources()} is called. Please consider adding {@code spring-boot-devtools} to your project instead as it provides this feature and many more.

parameter.name: additionalClasspathElements
parameter.description: Additional classpath elements that should be added to the classpath. An element can be a directory with classes and resources or a jar file.

parameter.name: agents
parameter.description: Path to agent jars.

parameter.name: arguments
parameter.description: Arguments that should be passed to the application.

parameter.name: classesDirectory
parameter.description: Directory containing the classes and resource files that should be used to run the application.

parameter.name: commandlineArguments
parameter.description: Arguments from the command line that should be passed to the application. Use spaces to separate multiple arguments and make sure to wrap multiple values between quotes. When specified, takes precedence over {@link #arguments}.

parameter.name: directories
parameter.deprecated: since 3.2.0 for removal in 3.4.0 in favor of 'additionalClasspathElements'
parameter.description: Additional directories containing classes or resources that should be added to the classpath.

parameter.name: environmentVariables
parameter.description: List of Environment variables that should be associated with the forked process used to run the application.

parameter.name: excludeGroupIds
parameter.description: Comma separated list of groupId names to exclude (exact match).

parameter.name: excludes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to exclude. The {@link Exclude} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: includes
parameter.description: Collection of artifact definitions to include. The {@link Include} element defines mandatory {@code groupId} and {@code artifactId} components and an optional {@code classifier} component. When configured as a property, values should be comma-separated with colon-separated components: {@code groupId:artifactId,groupId:artifactId:classifier}

parameter.name: jvmArguments
parameter.description: JVM arguments that should be associated with the forked process used to run the application. On command line, make sure to wrap multiple values between quotes.

parameter.name: mainClass
parameter.description: The name of the main class. If not specified the first compiled class found that contains a 'main' method will be used.

parameter.name: noverify
parameter.description: Flag to say that the agent requires -noverify.

parameter.name: optimizedLaunch
parameter.description: Whether the JVM's launch should be optimized.

parameter.name: profiles
parameter.description: The spring profiles to activate. Convenience shortcut of specifying the 'spring.profiles.active' argument. On command line use commas to separate multiple profiles.

parameter.name: project
parameter.description: The Maven project.

parameter.name: session
parameter.description: The current Maven session. This is used for toolchain manager API calls.

parameter.name: skip
parameter.description: Skip the execution.

parameter.name: systemPropertyVariables
parameter.description: List of JVM system properties to pass to the process.

parameter.name: testClassesDirectory
parameter.description: Directory containing the test classes and resource files that should be used to run the application.

parameter.name: workingDirectory
parameter.description: Current working directory to use for the application. If not specified, basedir will be used.
